headline: MacIntyre leads Tennessee past Ohio State in passing duel
body: Tennessee's air attack overwhelmed Ohio State's secondary in a Week 3 shootout that came down to execution in critical moments. MacIntyre completed 31 of 49 passes for 346 yards and three touchdowns, establishing early rhythm through the middle of the field and forcing the Ohio State defense into reactive coverage. The Buckeyes countered with Sayin's efficient if limited performance—25 for 40, 259 yards, one touchdown—but couldn't generate enough explosive plays to match Tennessee's sustained drive-and-score approach.

The turning point arrived in the third quarter when Tennessee's defense tightened coverage on Ohio State's receivers, particularly harassing Sayin on third and long situations. Smith's eight receptions for 110 yards gave Tennessee a reliable safety valve that Ohio State's Kelly and the secondary simply couldn't account for on every snap. By the final quarter, Tennessee had established enough of a cushion that even when Ohio State's defense stiffened, the Volunteers' lead held firm. The decisive margin—six points—reflected two offenses that executed in patches but never found complete rhythm simultaneously.

Tennessee's possession management and superior passing efficiency ultimately separated the teams. The Volunteers controlled time of possession through MacIntyre's willingness to take what the coverage gave him, while Ohio State's run game never found traction despite Judkins' touchdown. Tennessee's one turnover proved inconsequential; Ohio State's turnover-free performance couldn't compensate for falling short on third-down conversions and explosive pass plays. This was a game where the team that executed the passing game with greater consistency won on the road in a manner that felt convincing despite the final score's tightness.
turningPoint: Tennessee's secondary forced Sayin into uncomfortable throwing windows in the third quarter, triggering a run of three-and-outs that let the Volunteers extend their lead to double digits.

unsungHero.name: WR Smith (Tennessee)
unsungHero.why: Smith's eight catches for 110 yards provided MacIntyre's most reliable progression option and forced Ohio State into predictable coverage rotations, but Daune Morris earned POG honors despite Bishop's heavier workload overshadowing the receiving corps that opened lanes.
